message:
fix(raptorq): type-aware validation for e2e log schema config, loss, symbols, and outcome sections

validate_e2e_log_json now uses the same typed validation helpers
(validate_required_unsigned_integer_field, validate_required_string_field,
validate_required_bool_field, validate_required_number_field, and their
optional counterparts) that were introduced for the unit log schema.

Config section: adds type-checked validation for max_block_size,
min_overhead, block_count (unsigned integers) and repair_overhead
(number/float). Previously only symbol_size, seed, block_k, data_len
were validated, and all used the untyped null-or-missing check.

Loss section: adds type-checked validation for drop_count, keep_count
(required unsigned integers) and seed, drop_per_mille, burst_start,
burst_len (optional unsigned integers). kind is validated as a required
string.

Symbols section: new validation for the symbols.generated and
symbols.received sub-objects, each requiring total, source, and repair
as unsigned integers.

Outcome section: success validated as boolean, decoded_bytes as unsigned
integer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
